name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes mandatory workflows for finding and using skills, including using Read tool before announcing usage, following brainstorming before coding, and creating TodoWrite todos for checklists
capabilities: Checks for relevant skills before any task
Creates TodoWrite todos for all checklist items
author: DYAI2025
securitySummary: All 11 static findings are false positives. This skill is a pure markdown guidance document containing only workflow instructions for AI behavior. No executable code, network calls, filesystem access, or command execution. The static scanner flagged benign patterns: a GitHub URL in metadata (not a network call), git hash fields, YAML frontmatter, and common English words like 'skill' and 'source'. This is documentation-only content with zero security risk.
valueStatement: Tasks often repeat solved problems because AI agents skip established workflows. This skill ensures you always check for relevant skills before starting, follow mandatory workflows like brainstorming before coding, and track all checklist items with TodoWrite.
userTitle: Apply proven workflows to every task
limitations: Does not create or modify other skills
Does not execute code or scripts
Does not access external files or APIs

useCases.title: Learn proven workflows
useCases.description: New users learn established patterns for task execution from the start

useCases.title: Maintain consistency
useCases.description: Experienced developers maintain disciplined workflows across all projects

useCases.title: Enforce standards
useCases.description: Team leads ensure all tasks follow documented best practices

promptTemplates.title: Basic skill check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if a relevant skill exists for this task. If one exists, announce and use it.

promptTemplates.title: With checklist
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the [Skill Name] skill. Create a TodoWrite todo for each checklist item.

promptTemplates.title: Brainstorming first
promptTemplates.prompt: Before writing code, brainstorm the approach. Use the brainstorming skill if available.

promptTemplates.title: Verification step
promptTemplates.prompt: Verify the work is complete. Use the verification-before-completion skill if available.

bestPractices: Always announce which skill you are using before starting work.
Create a separate TodoWrite todo for each checklist item - never batch or skip.
Read the current version of skills - memory of previous versions may be outdated.
antiPatterns: Working through a checklist mentally without creating TodoWrite todos.
Skipping a skill because 'the task seems simple' or 'I remember how to do this.'
Treating specific instructions as permission to skip established workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all Claude tools?
faq.answer: Yes, it works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for workflow enforcement.

faq.question: What if no relevant skill exists?
faq.answer: Proceed with standard practices but consider documenting the approach as a new skill.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with other skills?
faq.answer: This skill helps you discover and use other skills. Chain it at the start of any task.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill only reads documentation. It does not access files, execute code, or send data.

faq.question: Why must I announce skill usage?
faq.answer: Announcing skill usage confirms you read it and helps users understand your process.

faq.question: How is this different from other productivity skills?
faq.answer: This skill is meta - it helps you find and use all other skills consistently.
